source: Engineering defaults pending corpus calibration. Tonal-hierarchy tie-breaker for relative major/minor (which share a diatonic collection, so correlation alone is weak): leading_tone is near-dispositive (the minor's raised 7th lies outside the shared collection) and weighted highest; tonic_triad and tonic salience follow tonal-hierarchy theory (Krumhansl). Signals are normalized by total weight; positive score favors the minor reading. near_tie_margin is the correlation gap below which the top candidate and its relative partner count as a tie worth breaking; decision_margin is the tie-break-score band that stays honestly ambiguous.
